name: NaturaLawn of America, Inc.

litigation_pending.case: Venisha Ford v. NaturaLawn of America, Inc.
litigation_pending.allegation: Violation of Telephone Consumer Protection Act
litigation_pending.status: Answer filed, denying liability with affirmative defenses; no class certified
litigation_pending.management_assessment: Potential material liability possible but not probable

validation_questions: Confirm Item 19 cohort size and definition with franchisor.
Confirm current auditor and most recent audit opinion.

internal_unresolveds.reason: TCPA litigation outcome unknown — class action potential could create material exposure
Enforcement of $500K minimum Gross Sales — some franchisees appear to operate well below threshold ($252K low)
Confidentiality clause scope and enforceability — may impede prospective franchisee due diligence
Advertising fee (1%) spending accountability — no fund audit or reporting disclosed; appears franchisor-retained
Due from related entities ($882K) — no formal repayment terms; 1% interest rate below market
Territory is 'exclusive' but franchisor may solicit/accept orders in territory without compensation
Item 7 notes required credit line of $150K-$250K; Item 9 references $75K-$125K credit line per FA §12L
Note 6 states 'no franchisor-owned outlets' but Table 4 shows 10 company-owned outlets operated by affiliated entities
